_doc: EXAMPLE run config for a bounded Agents-A1 shadow workload (M11, src/run_agents_a1_shadow_batch.py). PLACEHOLDER, not a secret and not a real internal host. Copy to a local (gitignored) config and edit for your own endpoint. The default deterministic smoke path needs none of the live fields. A genuinely LIVE run against Agents-A1 is HUMAN-GATED: the operator serves the GGUF endpoint first — the agent never serves models.

endpoint._model_doc: A GGUF chat endpoint returns OUTPUT TEXT ONLY — no router logits. So each record sets telemetry_missing=true and policy=null. Features are never fabricated.
endpoint._api_key_doc: Most local endpoints ignore this. Do NOT put a real secret here; use an env var in your own local config if your endpoint needs auth.

task_sources._doc: PUBLIC smoke batch — safe for committed samples and tests.
OPTIONAL local private queue — gitignored, real prompts, NEVER committed. Absent by default.

batch._doc: Bounded workload — start small. size is the per-run cap; cap is the hard maximum this config permits.

verifiers._doc: Reuse the M10 supervisor verifier toggles. Each produces an auto_outcome signal only; none is gold.

escalation._doc: When to flag a record for later HUMAN review. Advisory only; never sets a human outcome.

resume._doc: Resume/retry-safe: on re-run, prompt_ids already present in the run log are skipped and only missing tasks are appended.

run._doc: deterministic=true uses placeholder timestamps + the deterministic dry-run output so committed samples are reproducible. mode dry-run needs no network; live is HUMAN-GATED (operator serves the endpoint first).
run._paths_doc: All default outputs are under the gitignored private dir — raw prompt/output text stays local. Commit only aggregate-only or redacted artifacts.
